[In the opinion of BhaUotpala there is no order meant in res-
pect of the quarters occupied by Jupiter and' Venus. So either of
them can be in the east or west, but it is essential that they should
be in the 7th house from each other. He quotes tmnr,
Tipnn^rer etc. He has come to the conclusion that even the Sages are
not unanimous on this subject. Says TTWIT—
fzndt to TOJ n
Here this Sage introduces a new rule i.e. both Jupiter and Venus
being situated in the eastern horizon.
